title: rhus cotinoides
intro: Rhus cotinoides, also known as Poison Sumac, is a shrub native to the southeastern United States and the Gulf Coast. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Rhus cotinoides include treating fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis, as well as being used as a natural remedy for sore muscles and joints. Herbal preparations of Rhus cotinoides are often made into teas, salves, and tinctures, which can be used topically or taken internally to reap its medicinal benefits.
